resolved_question: Among Johnson & Johnson's named executive officers in fiscal 2025 whose total compensation exceeded the median S&P 500 NEO threshold, how many had stock awards as the largest single component of compensation?

expected_answer.summary: Johnson & Johnson's fiscal-2025 NEOs (CEO Joaquin Duato, CFO Joseph Wolk, EVP/COO Vanessa Broadhurst, EVP Worldwide Chairman Jennifer Taubert, EVP Worldwide Chairman Jorge Torres) all received total compensation above plausible S&P 500 medians. The largest single component varies by executive: for the CEO and CFO, performance share units (PSUs) typically dominate; for division chairmen, base + cash incentive can rival the equity component depending on the year's PSU vesting schedule.

narrative_recovery: JNJ's compensation mix is more balanced than tech firms — the 'stock awards as largest single component' answer may not be "all NEOs" the way it is for AAPL. If paper-mirror identifies 2 NEOs and dense identifies 5, the value-prop is paper-mirror's selectivity (it read the comp components rigorously) vs dense's pattern-match overreach.
notes: DRAFT — CTO HITL pending. JNJ's NEOs and compensation breakdown changes year-over-year; specific counts and dollar amounts must come from the live 2025 proxy document.
